name: RevitMCPCommandSet
description: Basic command collection for Revit AI assistance

commands.description: Displays a greeting dialog
get available family types
get current view elements
get current view info
get selected elements
Create point-based elements like furniture
Create line based element such as wall
Create surface-based elements like floors
Color elements by parameter value
Tag all the walls in the model
Deletes elements using ElementId
AI element filter for querying elements by criteria
Operate on elements (select, color, hide, isolate, etc)
Extract all rooms with detailed properties including area, volume, perimeter, and parameters
Calculate material quantities and takeoffs with area and volume calculations per material
Analyze model complexity with element counts by category, type, family, and level
Create grid system with smart spacing generation for project layout (alphabetic/numeric naming)
Create structural beam framing system with configurable spacing and direction
Create and place rooms at specified locations with custom names, numbers, and properties
Create tags for all rooms in the current view displaying room name and number
Create levels at specified elevations with automatic floor plan view generation
Execute dynamic C# code in Revit with access to the Document and Revit API
Create dimension annotations between elements or points in the current view
Get all instance and type parameters of Revit elements by their IDs
Set parameter values on Revit elements (instance and type parameters)
Move, rotate, mirror, or copy Revit elements
Create views (FloorPlan, CeilingPlan, Section, Elevation, 3D)
Create sheets with title blocks for documentation
Place views on sheets as viewports
Create text note annotations in views
Get project information including phases, worksets, links, and levels
Create schedule views with fields, filters, and sorting
Read schedule data or list all schedules in the project
Export schedule data to text/CSV file
List all project parameters (shared and non-shared) bound to categories in the current Revit project
Add a shared parameter from the shared parameter file to specified categories
List all worksets with properties including name, kind, open/editable status
Move elements to a different workset
Get all phases and phase filters from the project
Set created and/or demolished phase on elements
List all materials with properties including color, transparency, and asset availability
Get detailed structural, thermal, and appearance properties of a material
Get all warnings/errors in the current Revit model for quality auditing
Identify and optionally remove unused families, types, and materials
Batch rename views, sheets, or elements using find/replace or prefix/suffix rules
Load family files, list loaded families, or duplicate family types
List, reload, or unload linked Revit models
Create floor elements from boundary points or room boundaries
Create, apply, or list view filters for controlling element visibility
Copy elements between views within the same document
Create linear or radial arrays of elements
Export sheets/views to PDF, DWG, or IFC format
Sequentially renumber rooms, doors, windows, or parking elements by spatial order or custom sequence
Batch swap family types on multiple elements at once (e.g., change all doors from one type to another)
List, apply, or remove view templates on views for consistent drawing standards
Duplicate views with options: independent, dependent, or with detailing
Set per-element graphic overrides in a view (line color, fill color, transparency, halftone, lineweight)
Create 2D filled regions (hatched areas) in views with specified boundaries and fill patterns
List, create revisions, or add revisions to sheets for documentation tracking
Copy parameter values from a source element to multiple target elements (match properties)
Detect geometric intersections between element categories or specific elements for coordination
List, audit, and delete CAD imports and links from the model for cleanup
Comprehensive BIM model health audit with scoring and recommendations
Measure distance between elements, points, or mixed references
Import parameter values into elements from structured data with dry-run support
Create multiple sheets with title blocks and optional view placement
Create 3D view with section box fitted to selected elements with configurable offset
Bulk modify parameter values: set, prefix, suffix, find/replace, or clear on multiple elements
Duplicate sheets with all content: views, legends, schedules in exact positions
Auto-create callout, section, or elevation views from rooms with naming patterns
Align viewports across sheets to match a reference viewport position
Find elements contained within rooms, areas, or custom bounding boxes
Comprehensive family audit with health score, unused detection, and recommendations
Batch modify view range settings on multiple plan views at once
Transfer parameter values from a source element to multiple target elements
Export elements by category with selected parameters, filtering, and CSV/JSON output. Core SheetLink feature.
Manage project parameters (CRUD): list, create, delete, or modify category bindings. Inspired by DiRoots ParaManager.
Colorize elements by parameter value with auto/gradient/custom schemes and generate a legend view. Inspired by DiRootsOne OneFilter Visualize.
